rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery, To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Domco Products Texas Inc.¿s motion to compel further responses is granted as to special interrogatories 36 through 40 because plaintiffs¿ responses are insufficient. Defendant¿s motion to compel is granted as to form interrogatories 4.1 and 6.1 through 6.7 because plaintiffs never served any responses at all to those interrogatories. Plaintiffs have 15 days to serve responses. Defendant¿s motion to deem admitted requests for admission nos. 15 through 30 is granted. Plaintiffs failed to timely respond to those requests and subsequently never served responses in substantial compliance with C.C.P. section 2033.220. (C.C.P. section 2033.280(b), (c).) Defendant¿s request for sanctions of $1979.50 is granted. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate substantial justification. Defendant¿s motion as to all other discovery requests is off calendar as moot, as defendant¿s reply did not suggest that anything but the above-referenced discovery remained at issue. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Sears, Roebuck And Company
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 26, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the deposition of the person most qualified and custodian of records of Sears, Roebuck and Company. is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)
